summary: High‑energy set with standout Foolish Heart, Space→Other One→Wheel and fiery Shakedown Street
blurb: The second half erupts with a smoking Foolish Heart and an expansive Space → The Other One → Wheel suite, plus a monster Shakedown Street. Jerry’s leads are on fire, Brent’s keyboards drive the jams, and the band keeps tight momentum throughout.
review: On April 2, 1989 at Pittsburgh’s Civic Arena the Dead delivered a high‑octane performance that still feels fresh for fans today. The first set rolled along with bright renditions of Iko Iko, Dire Wolf and Brown Eyed Women, but it was the second set that truly ignited the crowd: Foolish Heart opened a blistering run into a soaring Space → The Other One → Wheel sequence, followed by an inspired Dear Mr. Fantasy and a rousing Hey Jude Finale before the band closed with Turn On Your Lovelight. Jerry Garcia was “on fire all night,” laying scorching leads on Shakedown Street and delivering gritty vocals that cut through the mix. Brent Mydland’s organ was warm and punchy, filling every groove from Lovelight to Fantasy, while Phil Lesh’s bass rumbled solidly under the band’s drive. Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann locked in with aggressive yet cohesive drumming, especially during the extended Space jam. The audience captured on the recommended front‑row tape brings the room’s electricity right into your headphones, making this show a must‑listen for anyone craving that late‑‘80s Dead vigor.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: The majority of recordings (5 positive, 2 mixed, 0 negative) praise the performance with words like 'smoking', 'on fire', 'monster', and highlight multiple songs. Mixed comments focus on recording fidelity rather than musical flaws, so a strong positive adjustment is warranted.

best_recording.reason: Front‑row audience tape with good clarity, captures the high‑energy set and key highlights (Foolish Heart, Space→Other One→Wheel, Shakedown Street) without major dropouts.

key_highlights: Foolish Heart ignites the second set
Space → The Other One → Wheel sequence erupts with intensity
Shakedown Street delivers a monster groove and scorching leads
Brent’s organ shines on Turn On Your Lovelight and Fantasy

band_performance.Jerry: Jerry was on fire all night, delivering scorching leads on Shakedown Street and Foolish Heart and a gritty vocal presence that cut through the mix.
band_performance.Phil: Phil’s bass provided a steady, rumbling foundation that drove the groove, especially evident during Shakedown Street.
band_performance.Bob: Bob added solid harmonies and rhythmic support throughout, enhancing the energy of the second‑set jams.
band_performance.Brent: Brent Mydland’s organ was warm, punchy and full of electric fills, shining on Turn On Your Lovelight, Fantasy and driving the Space → The Other One transition.
band_performance.Mickey: Mickey Hart locked in with Bill, supplying a steady pulse and lifting the extended Space jam to an above‑average level.
band_performance.Billy: Bill Kreutzmann kept an aggressive yet cohesive beat, propelling the high‑octane second set.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
